sections.title: Intake

sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Intersect governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, repository management, security initiatives, and management of critical processes for June 2026 to June 2027.
Recipient: Intersect / 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v

Deliverables: WP 1 - Intersect operations and ecosystem coordination: 6,000,000 ADA, WP 2 - Technical Stewardship, Incident Response & Coordination including core Cardano repos: 18,800,000 ADA, WP 3 - Management of critical processes: 600,000 ADA, Milestone-based drawdowns, evidence-backed reporting, transparent disclosures, and external assurance process

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect requests 25,400,000 ADA for its core operating model covering governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, and critical unowned processes from June 2026 to June 2027.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed budget is allocated as 6,000,000 ADA for operations and ecosystem coordination, 18,800,000 ADA for technical stewardship and incident response, and 600,000 ADA for management of critical processes.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Intersect states that its overall ask has been reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect states it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says it achieved the required 67% support threshold in the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and therefore advanced to on-chain Treasury Withdrawal submission.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states that the requested amount does not breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit for Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 at the time of submission.
missing: Independent evidence for: Intersect states that its overall ask has been reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity.
Independent evidence for: Intersect states it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.
Independent evidence for: Independent audits and assurance for the proposal will be provided by Appold, with possible review of deliverables, controls, spend validation, and milestone evidence.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Intersect requests 25,400,000 ADA for its core operating model covering governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, and critical unowned processes from June 2026 to June 2027." though 1 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Intersect states that its overall ask has been reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
